title: Integral Photography (Lippmann-style MLA capture + reconstruction)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in Lippmann 1908 integral photography and modern Fourier-integral photography literature.

E.description: Integral photography records a scene through a microlens array in front of (or near) the sensor; each microlens forms a small image of the exit pupil. The result is an elemental-image array whose pixel-for-pixel rearrangement produces multiple sub-aperture views that jointly encode 3D structure. Viewing through a matched MLA recreates the light field; digital reconstruction solves for parallax, depth, or view synthesis.
E.forward_model: y(i,j) = sum_k I_k(i - u_k, j - v_k) * mla_mask_k(i,j) + n(i,j)  where I_k is the k-th elemental image
E.world_state_x: Scene represented either as (a) elemental-image array {I_k}_{k=1..N_ml}, or (b) equivalent 4D light field L(u,v,s,t), or (c) 3D scene + depth map
E.observation_y: 2D sensor mosaic y in R^{H_s x W_s} encoding the tiled elemental images
physical_parameters_theta: MLA-sensor gap g (sets angular field), focal length f_ml
Spatial resolution per elemental image (L_x, L_y) (pixels under each microlens)

W.regime: Well-conditioned when MLA is rigidly calibrated and angular/spatial trade-off is sufficient (N_ml * L_x >= scene bandwidth). Fourier-mode integral photography trades angular for spatial resolution; reconstruction is linear with known MLA geometry.

C.solver_class: pixel rearrangement + sub-aperture extraction, iterative MLA super-resolution (ADMM), learned LF super-resolution applied to IP views
C.error_bound: |I_hat - I|_L2 <= C1 * sigma / sqrt(angular_samples) + C2 * mla_calibration_error + C3 * vignetting_residual
C.complexity: O(H_s * W_s) for rearrangement; O(N_iter * H * W * L_x * L_y) for super-resolution
